title: Cynophalla retusa
taxonomy: Cynophalla retusa is classified in the family Capparaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the caper family. This family is part of the larger order Brassicales, which includes other flowering plants like cabbage and mustard. Cynophalla retusa is native to tropical Africa and is a species of shrub or small tree. It belongs to the genus Cynophalla, which is a group of flowering plants characterized by their small, greenish-yellow flowers. The exact classification of Cynophalla retusa may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the family Capparaceae.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it a promising natural remedy for various ailments.
